live_registry.registry_refresh: scoreboard cache/refresh path is active and consulted during recommendation generation

verified_reason_or_thesis_fields_that_up: leader and score margin wording from score_diff
gate failure reasons such as live_warmup, near_resolution, stale_book, stale_game_state, edge_too_small

verified_runtime_cadence.conclusion: The active runtime does recalculate repeatedly during games, but the default 30-second loop is coarse for truly aggressive live trading.

important_live_factors_still_missing_or_: live lineup/order/batter identity changes are not clearly present in the active recommendation path
live batter-specific state is not clearly fed into current inference
live baserunner identity/speed/quality is not present beyond encoded base occupancy
live defensive substitutions and fielding alignment changes are not present
weather/park-state live changes are not present in the active path
roster availability changes beyond current pitcher/bullpen state are not clearly present
there is no proof here of pitch-by-pitch or sub-10-second recalculation cadence

strict_interpretation.why: Current code clearly rebuilds live game-state features and reruns inference during games, but it still operates on a relatively coarse loop and omits several richer live baseball factors that a stronger real-time model would incorporate.

trust_assessment.why: It is meaningfully more than a static pregame model because live baseball state changes feed probability inference, but the cadence and missing rich live factors argue for caution before calling it a fully mature true live model.
